summary: Matched the Dolphin SDK pad module by refactoring extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/pad/pad.c, promoting it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, and correcting sdata symbol sizes for CmdReadOrigin and CmdCalibrate. The core source changes replaced inline string literals used for SITransfer pad-origin commands with explicit global command buffers, simplified DoReset to a void helper, rewrote PADTypeAndStatusCallback control flow around ResettingChan/type/recalibration state, and restored a direct PADReset(0) path in PADRead for pending pads.
what_changed: Marked dolphin/pad/pad.c as Object(Matching, "dolphin/pad/pad.c") in configure.py instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Updated GALE01 symbols for pad command globals: CmdReadOrigin size changed from 0x2 to 0x1 and CmdCalibrate size changed from 0x2 to 0x3.
Added explicit global command arrays in pad.c: char CmdReadOrigin[1] = "A" and char CmdCalibrate[3] = "B\0\0".
Changed DoReset from returning int to returning void, removed unused local return plumbing, and made it operate directly on ResettingChan, ResettingBits, Origin, and SIGetTypeAsync.
Rewrote PADTypeAndStatusCallback with clearer chanBit/recalibrate/rc locals, early returns after DoReset paths, explicit type masking, and explicit use of PADEnable for Spec < 2.
Replaced multiple SITransfer calls that used string literals "A" or "B" with pointers to CmdReadOrigin or CmdCalibrate.
In PADRead, replaced a larger inline reset sequence guarded by a previous #if workaround with a direct PADReset(0) call when PendingBits contains the channel bit.
Added a small unused local stack array u8 _[8] in PADInit, likely to influence stack frame/layout for matching.
smart_moves: Using explicit global command buffers for the SI command bytes made the emitted data layout match the target and aligned with the corrected symbols.txt sizes.
Changing DoReset to void removed artificial return-value code and better matched how the helper is actually used.
The PADTypeAndStatusCallback rewrite preserved behavior while shaping compiler output through simpler temporaries, early returns, and direct use of ResettingChan.
Replacing string literals in SITransfer with named globals avoided anonymous literal placement differences and made the command data searchable and symbolized.
Switching PADRead back to PADReset(0) avoided a hand-expanded reset sequence that the diff notes produced many register swaps.
decomp_lessons: For SDK code, tiny command strings may need to be represented as named global arrays rather than inline string literals to match both codegen and sdata layout.
Symbol sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt must reflect the actual emitted object sizes; here a one-byte read-origin command and three-byte calibrate command were required.
Do not keep synthetic return values in static helpers if all call sites ignore the return; the original function signature may be void and extra return plumbing can prevent matching.
Early-return control flow can be a useful matching tactic when reducing register pressure and avoiding compiler-generated regswaps.
Sometimes the semantically obvious library/helper call, such as PADReset(0), matches better than a manually inlined equivalent sequence.
Small dummy stack locals can be used as a matching lever, but the evidence here only shows u8 _[8] was added, not the exact stack-layout reason.
naming_conventions: Global SDK/static data names use PascalCase or SDK-style names such as CmdReadOrigin, CmdCalibrate, ResettingChan, ResettingBits, RecalibrateBits, PendingBits, EnabledBits, Origin, Type, and Spec.
Callback names retain Dolphin PAD naming style, for example PADTypeAndStatusCallback, PADOriginCallback, PADProbeCallback, PADOriginUpdateCallback, and PADReceiveCheckCallback.

SI constants and helper names use SDK uppercase/macros or SI-prefixed function names such as SI_GC_WIRELESS, SI_WIRELESS_IR, SI_WIRELESS_RECEIVED, SITransfer, SIGetResponse, SISetCommand, and SIEnablePolling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote a file to Matching only after source and data-symbol layout have both been adjusted.
Use named global arrays for command byte sequences passed to SITransfer instead of string literals when literal pooling/data placement differs.
Adjust symbol object sizes to match exact array declarations: CmdReadOrigin[1] and CmdCalibrate[3].
Rewrite conditionals with early returns to influence register allocation in complex callbacks.
Prefer direct helper calls over hand-expanded equivalent logic when the helper call is what the target binary emits.
Add harmless stack padding/local arrays when needed for stack-frame matching, as seen with u8 _[8] in PADInit, though the PR excerpt does not prove the exact matching constraint.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR merged quickly with one issue comment counted, but no comment body was included in the slice, so reviewer rationale cannot be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other Dolphin SDK modules where inline string literals should be replaced by named sdata command arrays.
Search for symbol size mismatches involving string data in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Review remaining NonMatching Dolphin pad-related files after pad.c was marked Matching.
Look for prior or later PRs discussing dummy stack locals such as u8 _[8] for stack-frame matching.
